title: Identify points on a line
description: Identify the coordinates of points on a line and match a table of x- and y-coordinates to a line.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following graph, the ordered pairs from the table represent points on line $\blueD m$.

**Complete the missing values in the table.**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1951fcb6922a35afad67f55a2e772f30571a8afd)

$x$ | $y$ 
:-: | :-: 
$0$ | $0$
$2$ |[[☃ numeric-input 4]]
$5$ | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each  $(x, y)$ pair from the table represents a point on line $\blueD m$. 

The $x$ value represents a number on the horizontal axis.

The $y$ value represents a number on the vertical axis.
We can use the graph to see where each $x$ value given in the table intersects the $\blueD{\text{line}}$. Then we can determine the $y$ value at the point of intersection.

For example, the line intersects $x=0$ at $y=0$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e52f482c2c2a29e3c067bd60c6957ff4a5a16ab5)
The line intersects $\maroonD{x=2}$ at $\greenD{y=4}$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/851bab7c397b212074e43282d0b1bc17aa6f6db8)

The line intersects $\maroonD{x=5}$ at $\greenD{y=10}$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c0eb0b57fa3506d2af76af8c84e2f768a447b09f)


The following table represents the graph:

$x$ | $y$ 
:-: | :-: 
$0$ |  $0$
$2$ |$4$
$5$ |  $10$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which ordered pair is part of both line $\blueD{j}$ and line $\goldD{k}$?**


![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b7ad391f7382816e5ac2ce18525ba2ea544d6c6a)


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The only point that is part of line $\blueD j$ and line $\goldD k$ is the $\purpleD{\text{point}}$ where the $2$ lines intersect.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fc5e4e43e903c3f08efff3c3993c895e0465efbd)
The $x$-coordinate for the point of intersection is $\redD4$. The $y$-coordinate for the point is $\maroonD8$. 



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/943986ec5b289eedc5f71b74bc43bde2ea971918)
The ordered pair that is part of both line $j$ and line $k$ is $(4, 8)$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which ordered pair is part of both line $\goldD{a}$ and line $\greenD{c}$?**


![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a93674e97b8036ddb3d5974cd089a4c688ac574f)


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The only point that is part of line $\goldD a$ and line $\greenD c$ is the $\purpleD{\text{point}}$ where the $2$ lines intersect.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0f1357f349ae46020b51519cb4311f2a43324ca2)

The $x$-coordinate for the point of intersection is $\redD5$. The $y$-coordinate for the point is $\maroonD3$. 



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15492eaae9e97fd59c3ae8896972e0432fbb6e90)
The ordered pair that is part of both line $a$ and line $c$ is $(5, 3)$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Ordered pair $(2, 4)$ is part of which line?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b7ad391f7382816e5ac2ce18525ba2ea544d6c6a)


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An ordered pair is given in the form $(x, y)$. 

The $x$-coordinate represents a value on the horizontal axis.

The $y$-coordinate represents a value on the vertical axis.

The point matching the ordered pair  $(2, 4)$ is shown in the graph.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/44ecab2beb6d4eae0e937df4ba3bb63027874afc)
Ordered pair $(2, 4)$ is part of line $j$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Ordered pair $(3, 5)$ is part of which line?**


![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a93674e97b8036ddb3d5974cd089a4c688ac574f)


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An ordered pair is given in the form $(x, y)$. 

The $x$-coordinate represents a value on the horizontal axis.

The $y$-coordinate represents a value on the vertical axis.

The point matching the ordered pair  $(3, 5)$ is shown in the graph.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f646165400860e028070c2dbc058e6e0f2ab154d)
Ordered pair $(3, 5)$ is part of line $a$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**For any point on line $\greenD{n}$, the $y$-coordinate is [[☃ numeric-input 1]].**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b7ad391f7382816e5ac2ce18525ba2ea544d6c6a)
hints:
The $x$-coordinate represents a value on the horizontal axis.

The $y$-coordinate represents a value on the vertical axis.

Because line $n$ is horizontal, the $y$-coordinate is the same for every $x$-coordinate. 

The ordered pairs for $3$ points on line $n$ are shown in the graph. Each point has a different $x$-coordinate, but the $y$-coordinates are all the same.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e0d55645f6e674c2ed22b993132e8e337f7bb45)
For any point on line $n$, the $y$-coordinate is $6$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**For any point on line $\greenD{c}$, the $y$-coordinate is [[☃ numeric-input 1]].**



![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a93674e97b8036ddb3d5974cd089a4c688ac574f)
hints:
The $x$-coordinate represents a value on the horizontal axis.

The $y$-coordinate represents a value on the vertical axis.

Because line $c$ is horizontal, the $y$-coordinate is the same for every $x$-coordinate. 

The ordered pairs for $3$ points on line $c$ are shown in the graph. Each point has a different $x$-coordinate, but the $y$-coordinates are all the same.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/430c24c97f993fee018ca56db2f5ab1cf9c4c935)
For any point on line $c$, the $y$-coordinate is $3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The $x$ and $y$ values shown  in the table represent points on one of the lines shown in the graph below.

$x$ | $y$ 
:-: | :-: 
$1$ | $5$
$3$ |$7$
$5$ | $9$





![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b7ad391f7382816e5ac2ce18525ba2ea544d6c6a)


**Which line do the values in the table represent? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $x$-coordinate represents a value on the horizontal axis.

The $y$-coordinate represents a value on the vertical axis.

The $x$ and $y$ pairs from the table are shown on the graph.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fde7951b78c8de88abb6765ea8d538ec4987835c)
The values shown in the table represent line $k$.
